title: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) Tourism and Travel
content: The BBA in Travel and Tourism Management specialisation is tailored for students passionate about the vibrant and ever-evolving travel and tourism industry. This program provides a comprehensive understanding of tourism principles, destination management, hospitality operations, and travel agency management. Students will explore emerging trends such as sustainable tourism, digital marketing in tourism, and experiential travel, gaining insights into consumer behaviour and service excellence. The curriculum covers various facets, including tour operations, airline ticketing, hotel management, and event planning. With India's rich cultural heritage and burgeoning tourism sector, graduates will find diverse opportunities in hotels, resorts, travel agencies, tour operating companies, event management firms, and government tourism organisations, contributing to the growth and development of this significant economic contributor. Course Category | Credits | Common Core | 54 | Specialisation Core | 18 | Specialisation Elective | 18 | VAD | 8 | AEC | 12 | Community Service Project | 2 | Internship | 5 | Industrial Research Project | 3 | Total | 120 | XII with minimum 50% marks in Aggregate. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) BBA (Tourism & Travel) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 01 Excel as skilled service professionals in travel agencies, tour operations, and hospitality sectors, delivering exceptional customer experiences and managing daily operations effectively. 02 Champion responsible tourism practices and cultural sensitivity, contributing to sustainable destination development and community engagement initiatives within the tourism ecosystem. 03 Adapt to emerging trends and digital innovations in the travel and tourism industry, pursuing continuous professional growth to advance into supervisory or specialized marketing/ operations roles. 01 Evaluate comprehensive travel and tourism packages, services, and marketing strategies, considering market trends, customer needs, and ethical tourism practices. 02 Manage various operational aspects of the tourism and hospitality sector, including destination management, tour operations, and customer relationship management, ensuring high-quality service delivery. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 XII with minimum 50% marks in Aggregate. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) BBA (Tourism & Travel) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level
